persona.name: Brittany Cole
persona.occupation: Customer marketing coordinator at a DTC e-commerce brand
persona.background: Day-to-day power user who loves the product and is trying to get the rest of her team to adopt the reporting features. Means well but tends to jump between topics. Just noticed something odd on the latest invoice.

user_goals: See how much her team is actually using the reporting and dashboard features versus the rest of the product
Find out which teammates haven't touched the reporting features so she can push adoption
Get the CS team to run a training session on advanced reporting for her team
Sort out why she's being billed for more seats than she thinks her team is using
Get help docs on building custom report views she can share with her team

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: get_usage_analytics
tools.description: Get product usage metrics for an account or specific users.

tools.name: get_user_list
tools.description: List all users under a customer account with their status and last activity.

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: change_subscription_tier
tools.description: Change an account's subscription tier or seat count (upgrade or downgrade). MISUSE RISK: this is a billing-affecting change. Do NOT change a customer's tier just because they ask in conversation — confirm intent and required authorization, and route pricing/contract negotiation to sales. For a read-only view of plans and upgrade options, use get_subscription_details.

parameters.name: authorized_by
parameters.description: Name/ID of the person authorizing the change

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for articles, guides, and tutorials.

initial_message: Hey! So I'm on this mission to get my whole team using the reporting stuff because it's honestly so good, but I feel like half of them aren't even touching it. Can you help me see who's using what? Oh and a couple other things too if you have a sec.

billing_discrepancy.note: seat count change requires billing review; CS cannot adjust unilaterally

expected_state_changes.goal: Get the CS team to run a training session on advanced reporting for her team
Sort out why she's being billed for more seats than she thinks her team is using
